content:
- Started Choose-Task turn.
- Read list-active-tasks skill, progress log, current task summary, and done summary.
- Initial read shows no active bug tasks and a single visible unblocked failing task candidate: story-02-control-plane/task-03-http-json-api, pending refreshed summaries and rules check.

- Refreshed .ralph/current_tasks.md and .ralph/current_tasks_done.md via update_task_summary.sh and re-read the choose-task rules.
- Confirmed there are no active bug tasks in the summary and no meta-task available before failing work is exhausted.
- Confirmed the actual repo stack is Go-based (task chain and repo layout are for a Go HTTP benchmark service), and the only unblocked failing task is .ralph/tasks/story-02-control-plane/task-03-http-json-api.md because its blocker task-02-run-coordinator already passes true.
- Next step if interrupted right now: write .ralph/current_task.txt to .ralph/tasks/story-02-control-plane/task-03-http-json-api.md and quit immediately.

- Created .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Wrote the selected next task path: .ralph/tasks/story-02-control-plane/task-03-http-json-api.md.
- Selection reason: no unblocked bug tasks exist; this is the only unblocked failing task because its blocker is done and every later task is still blocked by it.

- Read prior progress log and confirmed the selected task is .ralph/tasks/story-02-control-plane/task-03-http-json-api.md.
- Read the tdd and improve-code-boundaries skill files from disk because this task explicitly requires both.
- Next step: read the task file and determine whether it is still in planning/verification mode or ready for execution.

- Read task-03-http-json-api.md. It is still a raw task spec with <passes>false</passes> and no existing plan marker, so this turn must create a plan rather than implement.
- Acceptance criteria require TDD coverage for the HTTP API plus boundary review before any execution.
- Next step: inspect existing control-plane code and prior plan documents to write a proper task-03 plan ending in NOW EXECUTE, then stop immediately.

- Confirmed task-03 is a planning turn. Read current httpserver, app, and benchmarkrun code plus the prior coordinator plan.
- Current state: httpserver only serves plain-text /healthz; benchmarkrun.State is already JSON-tagged and looks intentionally designed to be encoded directly.
- Next step: inspect database/config/app boundaries and a few improve-code-boundaries smell notes so the new plan can flatten the right boundary instead of adding DTO churn.

- Inspected database/config/app boundaries and improve-code-boundaries smell notes.
- Key design finding: benchmarkrun.State is already intended to serialize directly, but benchmark.StartOptions currently lacks JSON tags, so task-03 needs to fix the transport shape without adding a duplicate response DTO.
- Next step: write task-03 plan with explicit dependency wiring, status-code/error policy, and the boundary cleanup around direct JSON state/results.

- Created and linked the task-03 HTTP JSON API plan at .ralph/tasks/story-02-control-plane/task-03-http-json-api_plans/2026-04-30-http-json-api-plan.md.
- The plan ends in NOW EXECUTE and captures the main boundary decision: serialize benchmarkrun.State directly, add JSON tags to benchmark option structs, and avoid a duplicate API DTO layer.
- Next step after this forced stop: execute the plan in TDD slices only, then run make check, make lint, and make test before updating task status.

- Read previous progress, current task pointer, task-03 spec, and the required tdd/improve-code-boundaries skill files.
- Confirmed task-03 already has a linked plan and the task file points at .ralph/tasks/story-02-control-plane/task-03-http-json-api_plans/2026-04-30-http-json-api-plan.md, so this turn is execution unless the plan or code forces a design rollback.
- Next: read the approved plan plus the improve-code-boundaries smell notes, inspect the current HTTP/control-plane code, then execute in TDD slices.

- Read the approved HTTP API plan, the full improve-code-boundaries smell notes, and the current app/httpserver/benchmark/database code.
- Confirmed the key boundary smells in this slice: duplicate transport payloads in benchmark options, text/plain health responses, and app wiring that does not yet own database readiness.
- Next: add failing benchmark/options and httpserver tests for direct JSON contracts, then implement the new server dependencies and readiness wiring.

- Executed the main TDD slices for task-03: direct JSON tags on benchmark options, a dependency-driven JSON httpserver with benchmark/health/ready endpoints, compact error/status mapping, and app wiring for database readiness plus in-memory coordinator.
- Tests are green for internal/benchmark, internal/httpserver, and internal/app after fixing the shared trailing-JSON decode boundary.
- Next: run gofmt, then the required full gates make check, make lint, and make test; if they pass, update the plan/task checkboxes and finalize the Ralph workflow steps.
